key_points: Erbium lasers (Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) demonstrate effective and selective caries ablation, smear layer removal, and improved adhesion to dental substrates.

In endodontics, laser-activated irrigation protocols such as photon-induced photoacoustic streaming (PIPS) and shock wave-enhanced emission photoacoustic streaming (SWEEPS) enhance irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption.
In prosthodontics and orthodontics, erbium lasers allow minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets while preserving underlying structures.
In periodontology and peri-implantitis, lasers improve debridement, support regeneration, and enhance wound healing.
Photobiomodulation (PBM/LLLT) contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair in dental contexts.
Clinical outcomes vary with wavelength, energy settings, and clinical protocols, underscoring the need for standardization.
The review is aligned with the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers, providing a framework for globally consistent clinical practice.
Further high-quality randomized controlled trials are necessary to define standardized laser protocols across dental specialties.

outcomes.result: Erbium lasers show effective and selective caries removal with smear layer elimination and improved adhesion
outcomes.clinical_significance: Minimally invasive alternative to conventional rotary instrumentation

outcomes.measured: Endodontic irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption
outcomes.result: PIPS and SWEEPS protocols enhance irrigant penetration into dentinal tubules and disrupt biofilm
outcomes.clinical_significance: Improved disinfection of root canal system compared to conventional irrigation

outcomes.measured: Debonding of prosthetic and orthodontic elements
outcomes.result: Erbium lasers enable minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets with preservation of underlying structures
outcomes.clinical_significance: Reduced risk of enamel or substrate damage during debonding

outcomes.result: Lasers improve debridement, support regeneration, and enhance wound healing in periodontal and peri-implant therapy
outcomes.clinical_significance: Adjunctive benefit to conventional periodontal treatment

outcomes.measured: Pain reduction and tissue repair via photobiomodulation
outcomes.result: PBM contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair
outcomes.clinical_significance: Improved patient comfort and biological healing

notes: Narrative review linked to the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers. No original clinical data; synthesizes existing literature from PubMed, Scopus, and Web of Science. No specific laser parameters reported as the review covers multiple applications and wavelengths without detailing individual protocol parameters.
source_citation: Grzech-Leśniak, K. (2026). Lasers in Dentistry - Overview Based on FDI Policy Statement. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2025.109369
clinical_indication: Multiple dental applications: caries removal, endodontic disinfection, periodontal therapy, peri-implant therapy, prosthetic and orthodontic debonding, photobiomodulation for pain and tissue repair
target_tissue: dentin, enamel, pulp, gingiva, bone, peri-implant tissue
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (power, fluence, pulse duration, frequency) are reported in the abstract; full text may contain more detail but was not available.
Conflicts of interest and funding sources not declared in the available metadata.
Publication year listed as 2026 but DOI suggests 2025 publication; may be ahead-of-print.
As a narrative review, selection bias in included studies cannot be assessed.
